config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan সমাজ/masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa batas, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat penghormatan pada hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankannya begitu saja. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi martabat dan kebebasannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should come first in many cases, but not at the expense of basic individual rights. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, because a society can fall apart if everyone only insists on personal freedom. But I do not accept the idea that society should automatically override the individual, since that can easily excuse abuse of power and silence ordinary people. The best approach is balance: protect core rights firmly, while allowing reasonable limits when they are truly necessary for the public good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, padahal justru minoritas dan orang biasa yang paling rentan dirugikan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, seperti keamanan, ketertiban, dan gotong royong, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar seenaknya. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang mudah menyalahgunakan nama masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have priority over the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in China, I understand the appeal of collective stability and why people may accept some limits on personal freedom for public order, but I have also seen how the phrase "for the good of society" can be used too easily to justify overreach and silence ordinary people. Individual rights should be the baseline, especially for basic dignity, speech, and protection from arbitrary power, while social interests should only limit them in narrow and clearly necessary cases. A healthy society is not one where the individual is sacrificed first, but one where the collective is strong because people's rights are respected.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih diprioritaskan daripada hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi dalih untuk membungkam yang lemah, padahal justru hak minoritas dan warga biasa yang paling perlu dilindungi dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama seperti keamanan, ketertiban, dan gotong royong memang penting, tetapi nilainya tidak boleh dipakai untuk menginjak martabat dan kebebasan dasar orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu duluan, melainkan yang kuat karena hak setiap orang dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not have automatic priority over the rights of the individual, even if I understand why many people in China value stability and collective responsibility. From my perspective, once “society first” becomes a general principle, it is very easy for authorities or majorities to excuse overreach and treat ordinary people as expendable. Social order matters, but it should be built on firm protections for basic rights, especially dignity, speech, and protection from arbitrary power. If those rights are weak, then what is called the public good can quickly become just a tool for control.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
